facade_description: 207 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick (upper), painted (ground) in a buff/golden-yellow brick upper, dark grey commercial ground floor palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s
